- World model: The light green strip is attached to the charcoal as a tail (like the yellow tail in Level 1). When charcoal moves, the tail moves with it. In Level 1, tail was on right, goal was move left. In Level 2, tail is on left, so goal might be to move RIGHT to the rightmost position.
- Goal model: Possibly align charcoal with the light green strip at cols 8-9. From cols 2-5, one right click would put it at cols 6-9, overlapping the green strip.
- Plan: Move charcoal all the way right (4 clicks from cols 6-9 to cols 22-25).

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! The top charcoal changed from 32px to 33px, and its boundary changed from [[0, 32], [0, 63]] to [[0, 31], [0, 63]]. So the top charcoal moved LEFT by 1 column (from cols 32-63 to cols 31-63).
So clicking the blue square at rows 16-19 (above the upper black bar) moves the TOP charcoal LEFT, not the bottom charcoal.
This means:
- Blue at rows 16-19 (above upper black bar): moves top charcoal LEFT
- Blue at rows 24-27 (below upper black bar): probably moves top charcoal RIGHT
- Blue at rows 36-39 (above lower black bar): moves bottom charcoal LEFT
- Blue at rows 44-47 (below lower black bar): moves bottom charcoal RIGHT
So there are TWO charcoals that can be moved independently! The top charcoal and the bottom charcoal.
In Level 1, there was only one charcoal. In Level 2, there are two charcoals.
Maybe the goal is to move both charcoals to specific positions. Let me check the top charcoal's range and try to figure out what the goal is.
The top charcoal is at cols 31-63 (33 pixels). Let me try moving it to see its range.
Actually, wait. The top charcoal has 33 pixels and is at row 0, cols 31-63. That's a 1x33 strip. The bottom charcoal has 16 pixels and is at rows 52-57, cols 10-13. That's a 4x4-ish shape.
Let me try moving the top charcoal to see its range and what happens.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Top charcoal moved LEFT when clicking blue at rows 16-19
# Try moving it all the way left
print("Moving top charcoal all the way left...")
for i in range(10):
    action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 18, 'col': 2}])
    seg = current_frame.segmentation
    for n in seg['nodes']:
        if n['color'] == 'c' and n['pixels'] > 20:
            cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
            print(f"  After click {i+1}: top charcoal cols = {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}, pixels={n['pixels']}")
    if last_action_result.get('level_completed') or last_action_result.get('done'):
        print("  Level completed!")
        break
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
